summary.intent: Build a full browser-in-browser feature for the Moltis web UI, allowing users to view live CDP screencasts of browser sessions, interact via mouse/keyboard/scroll, manage session history, and share browser cookies between the UI and agent sessions.
summary.outcome: Successfully shipped PR #531 with 60+ commits: live CDP screencasting, full mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction, URL autocomplete with Google suggestions, SQLite session history with action logs, per-agent browser profiles with shared-cookie tab reuse, 13 Rust unit tests and 6 Playwright e2e regression tests. Several critical bugs were fixed iteratively through live user testing.

repo: axum 0.8 uses `{param}` path syntax, not `:param` — panics at startup if wrong
biome version mismatch: `npx biome` = 0.3.3 (old), system `biome` = 2.4.6; local-validate.sh uses system version, causing repeated CI failures when using npx to auto-fix
WebSocket broadcast has a subscription filter — clients must explicitly subscribe to event types in websocket.js `subscribeEvents` list or frames are silently dropped
BrowserTool and RealBrowserService had completely separate BrowserManager instances, so agent-created sessions were invisible to the UI API
The service-traits crate cannot import sqlx types; use concrete types in gateway crate with trait default methods for optional capabilities
GPG-signed commits require YubiKey tap — background bash tasks reliably time out waiting for it; must use foreground commits
Chrome's `SingletonLock` is a symlink to the container hostname; `exists()` follows symlinks and returns false for dangling ones — use `symlink_metadata()` instead
Apple Container browser profiles must use `--restore-session-cookies` Chrome flag plus `--single-process-mojo-reservation` to persist session cookies across container restarts
Same profile_id → new tab in existing browser (shared cookies); different profile_id → new container (isolated cookies)
CDP `Page.startScreencast` relay task exits immediately if `tx.send()` finds zero receivers; must drop frames silently instead of exiting to handle the subscribe-after-start race
CDP `mouseWheel` event requires BOTH `deltaX` and `deltaY` fields always present or Chrome rejects the event entirely
Action name Display format includes parameters: `mouse_input(x=129, y=231)` not `mouse_input` — exact string matching always fails for parameterized actions
canvas ref callback (`canvasRefCallback`) needed instead of `useEffect([])` for conditionally-rendered canvas elements — effect runs once on mount before canvas exists
BrowserTool sandbox mode comes from SandboxRouter, not from `[tools.browser] sandbox` config — config only applied to UI path; tool path needs explicit override wiring in server.rs
Rapid stop/start_screencast cycles overwhelm CDP in containers; screencasts should never be stopped on session switch, only on explicit close

code.finding: Relay task exited when tx.send() found zero subscribers — race condition where first CDP frame arrived before UI subscribed
LLMs send explicit null for all optional fields; serde #[serde(default)] only handles missing keys, not null values — must strip nulls from JSON object before deserialization
req_session_id variable became unused after moving session logging to action hook — compiler warning
browser.screencast.frame was missing from subscribeEvents list — all screencast frames silently dropped by broadcast filter
profile_id was parameter of get_or_create but used inside launch_sandboxed_browser sub-function — needed threading through launch_browser intermediate function
OnceCell action hook was being set at startup via manager_if_ready() which returned None since manager is lazy-initialized; hook must be set inside the manager() init closure
screencasting.value signal captured in stale closure in mouse/wheel handlers; wheel events were silently dropped when screencasting.value was false after session switch without sendStartScreencast
CHROME_FLAGS env var passes additional Chrome flags to browserless/chrome container; used to add stealth and cookie-persistence flags
browser_tool created before db_pool — needed Arc<RealBrowserService> reference kept separate from Arc<dyn BrowserService> to call concrete set_session_store() after db_pool is available

workflow: For GPG-signed commits in long sessions, always use foreground Bash tool — background tasks timeout before YubiKey tap can happen
CDP screencast frame metadata contains scroll_offset_x/y and device dimensions — use these instead of polling get_url and evaluate for scroll position
CDP Page.frameNavigated event eliminates need for URL polling entirely — include URL in screencast frame payload on navigation
biome format --write must be run with the system binary (not npx) to match what local-validate.sh checks
Chrome in containers needs: --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled, realistic user agent without HeadlessChrome, stealth JS to patch navigator.webdriver
Multiple concurrent Chrome tabs on LinkedIn exhaust container memory; sandbox=false (host mode) is more reliable for JS-heavy sites

friction: YubiKey GPG signing blocked ~10+ commits — background tasks consistently timed out; had to retry repeatedly after user tap
biome version mismatch (npx 0.3.3 vs system 2.4.6) caused 4+ failed CI runs despite local --write passing
ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED in Apple Container on first navigate is a persistent flakiness issue — affects agent reliability
LinkedIn anti-bot detection serves sign-in page even when cookies are present in containerized Chrome
Chrome containers became completely unresponsive with multiple LinkedIn tabs — all actions timed out with no error logs
axum 0.8 path syntax change (:param → {param}) caused a runtime panic instead of compile error
Action hook registration timing issue (OnceCell lazy init) meant session history was empty for several test cycles before root cause found
Dangling SingletonLock symlink caused Chrome startup hang for 45+ seconds with no clear error — required manual cleanup
BrowserTool sandbox override not wired until very end of session — [tools.browser] sandbox=false config was ignored for all agent browser calls
open_items: Container memory/disk usage and up/down status display in Settings > Sandboxes UI (deferred as separate issue)
WebRTC upgrade path using neko/GStreamer for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth improvement (documented in PR, deferred)
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast (eliminating base64 overhead) — identified but not implemented
Cookie persistence e2e tests — planned but only partial unit tests added; full integration test with login→restart→verify not done
Per-session browser profile config in moltis.toml to allow named profiles for specific agent workflows
Chrome stealth measures (--disable-blink-features, navigator.webdriver patch) added at end of session — needs validation that LinkedIn detection is actually bypassed
Scrollbar on canvas shows page scroll position but has no drag interaction — can only click track, not drag thumb
Session action log in History tab shows raw action names but no screenshots/thumbnails for visual review
Container resource limits (--memory flag) not implemented — containers can exhaust host RAM with heavy pages
